About Observable Compute Foundation

Observable Compute Foundation is a South Dakota 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to independent AI research, expanding technology access in rural and underserved communities, and developing free educational resources for workers affected by AI-driven displacement.

Our Mission

We study what AI systems actually do — measurable behavior, documented function, no hype. Our research focuses on the communities most affected by AI-driven workforce displacement: rural towns, tribal nations, and economically vulnerable populations that receive limited attention from larger institutions.

Everything we publish is free and open access. We believe the communities most impacted by AI deserve the best information about it.

What We Do

  • Conduct original AI research on observable function, workforce displacement, and rural economic impact — all published freely through SSRN and our website
  • Provide free AI literacy resources including practical guides, tool directories, and educational primers
  • Deliver community technology education through in-person workshops at public libraries, tribal colleges, and community centers across western South Dakota
  • Track terminology evolution across research, legal, industry, and public domains through our Living Lexicon
  • Publish quarterly reports with transparent methodology and open data

501(c)(3) Nonprofit Status

Observable Compute Foundation is a South Dakota nonprofit corporation with IRS 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status.

  • EIN: 41-4747049
  • Incorporated: February 23, 2026
  • 501(c)(3) Determination: May 6, 2026
  • Location: Rapid City, South Dakota

Funding & Transparency

All funding sources and expenditures are documented publicly.

Current Funding

  • Founder contribution: $305 (filing fees)
  • Grants: Applications in progress
